summary: Iteration 12 — Selective consumption of uploaded batches by RUT jobs (bug fix verification). 11/11 backend tests passed (100%). Code inspection confirms _consume_uploads() in server.py:10282 was rewritten with selective pruning kwargs (used_proxy_raws, used_ua_strings, pending_leads_path); proxies/UAs branches use 'remaining = [it for it in items if it.strip() not in used_*_set]' + update_one (NOT delete_many); data_file branch uses shutil.copyfile(pending_leads.xlsx → current_fp). real_user_traffic.py captures used_proxy_set + used_ua_set per visit and persists them on finish; post-finish hook reads them from the DB record and forwards to _consume_uploads. End-to-end: created 4 uploads (5 proxies, 5 UAs, 5-row XLSX, automation_json), ran a 2-visit RUT job referencing all of them, waited for terminal state, verified via GET /api/uploads + direct DB peek that ALL 4 batches still exist with items[] arrays of length 3-5 (≤5, ≥3 — proving selective prune; the OLD bug would have wiped them entirely). automation_json template untouched as expected (reusable library, not in consume_upload_ids).

minor.issue: After selective prune, the upload doc's `items` array AND `count` field are updated correctly, but the LEGACY `item_count` field is NOT refreshed. Since GET /api/uploads response model maps doc['item_count'] → response.item_count (server.py:10025), the API/UI continues to show the original count (e.g. 5) even after items[] has been pruned to 3. The next RUT run will correctly load the reduced items via _load_upload_items (which reads items[] directly), so functional behaviour is correct — only the displayed count is stale. Fix: in /app/backend/server.py:10330 and :10347, also set `item_count: len(remaining)` alongside `count` (or replace `count` with `item_count`).

action_items: (MINOR) Update _consume_uploads in /app/backend/server.py:10330 + :10347 to also write `item_count: len(remaining)` so the UI / GET /api/uploads response shows the post-prune count, not the stale original. Currently it writes only `count` while the API response model reads `item_count`. Functional behaviour is unaffected — items[] is correctly pruned and the next RUT job will load the reduced set.
critical_code_review_comments: _consume_uploads selective-prune logic is sound: proxies/UAs branches build `remaining = [it for it in items if it.strip() not in used_*_set]`, call update_one with the trimmed list when remaining is non-empty, and delete_one only when remaining is empty — exactly matching the user requirement (Roman Urdu: 1000 proxy upload, only used remove ho — baqi saved rahe).

data_file branch correctly uses shutil.copyfile(pending_leads.xlsx → current_fp) to OVERWRITE the saved file with only the rows NOT submitted, so re-running a job won't re-attempt already-completed leads. When pending_rows == 0 (everything consumed) it deletes the file + batch. When pending_leads_path is missing (job died early), the batch is left untouched — preserving the user's data on a failed run.

automation_json upload type is intentionally excluded at job creation (server.py:3551 'NOT added to consume_upload_ids — automation templates are reusable across campaigns, NOT auto-deleted'). Verified by code inspection + e2e test (template still present in GET /api/uploads after job).

Per-visit tracking in real_user_traffic.py:1007 + 1016 adds proxy.raw + UA to used sets BEFORE the actual page.goto attempt — meaning even visits that fail at the proxy-connect stage will mark those proxies/UAs as 'attempted'. Correct semantics: a proxy that was tried (and failed) shouldn't be retried in the next run; the user expectation is 'remove items I touched'.
MINOR — `item_count` vs `count` field mismatch in the prune writer is the only blemish (see backend_issues.minor).

MINOR — _consume_uploads catches the outer exception and only logs a warning. If a partial prune fails mid-iteration (e.g., DB blip after proxies pruned but before UAs), there's no compensation — the next run will see partially-pruned state. Acceptable for an MVP, but consider wrapping each upload-id prune in its own try/except so one failure doesn't abort the rest.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration12_rut_selective_upload_consume.py (new — 11 tests, 5 code-shape + 4 upload-smoke + 2 end-to-end inc. live RUT job + DB peek)
test_credentials: Admin: admin@trackmaster.local / admin123. Dynamic user TEST_rut12_*@example.com / Passw0rd!.
seed_data_creation: Per session: 1 fresh registered user (real_user_traffic + links features enabled), 1 link (offer_url=https://example.com), 4 uploads (5-line proxies batch, 5-line UA batch, 5-row XLSX data_file, 1 automation_json template). 1 RUT job (2 visits, dummy proxies). All test data prefixed with TEST_ and isolated per-user.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run with: cd /app/backend && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://project-track-8.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest tests/test_iteration12_rut_selective_upload_consume.py -v. Total runtime ~2:15. The 2 e2e tests block ~120s waiting for the RUT job's dummy-proxy visits to fail-fast through the 45s page.goto timeout × 2 visits × concurrency=2 + post-finish hook + 5s settle. If you want to re-run only the code-shape tests (no live job), pass `-k TestConsumeUploadsCodeShape or TestUploadEndpoints` (~2s). The DB peek test imports server.get_user_db so MONGO_URL/DB_NAME from /app/backend/.env must be readable. Stale `item_count` field is a known minor — see backend_issues.minor.
rca of the issue: Original bug (pre-fix): _consume_uploads called user_db['uploaded_resources'].delete_many({'id': {'$in': upload_ids}}) on every job finish, wiping the entire batch even when only a tiny subset of items had been picked by the run. Fix verified in this iteration: (a) real_user_traffic.process_one now adds proxy.raw → used_proxy_set and ua → used_ua_set on every visit attempt (line 1007/1016); (b) at job end RUT_JOBS[job_id] is updated with used_proxy_raws + used_ua_strings (line 1785-1786) and persisted to the DB record; (c) post-finish hook (line 1804-1828) fetches these from db.real_user_traffic_jobs and forwards them to _consume_uploads; (d) _consume_uploads (server.py:10282) now BUILDS REMAINING = items minus used_set per upload and either update_ones with the trimmed list (non-empty remaining) or delete_ones (empty remaining). data_file branch overwrites the saved file with pending_leads.xlsx (rows NOT submitted) and only deletes when pending_rows == 0. automation_json type is excluded at job creation (server.py:3551 — never reaches the consume hook). End-to-end verified by running a 2-visit RUT job with 5/5/5/1 inputs and asserting all 4 batches survive with reduced items[] in the DB and via GET /api/uploads.
